tool.name: HAL maf2hal
tool.command: maf2hal #if $format: --format '$format' #end if #if $refGenome: --refGenome '$refGenome' #end if #if $targetGenomes: --targetGenomes '$targetGenomes' #end if --mmapFileSize $mmapFileSize #if $settings.cache == "custom" #if $settings.memory.hdf5InMemory --hdf5InMemory #else --hdf5CacheBytes $settings.memory.hdf5CacheBytes --hdf5CacheMDC $settings.memory.hdf5CacheMDC --hdf5CacheRDC $settings.memory.hdf5CacheRDC --hdf5CacheW0 $settings.memory.hdf5CacheW0 #end if --hdf5Chunk $settings.hdf5Chunk --hdf5Compression $settings.hdf5Compression #end if '$in_maf_file' out_hal_file.hal

param.name: in_maf_file
param.label: Input MAF file to import into HAL database
Reference genome
param.help: Name of reference genome in MAF (first found if empty)

param.label: Target genome
param.help: Comma-separated (no spaces) list of target genomes (others are excluded) (vist all if empty)

param.label: mmap HAL file initial size
param.help: mmap HAL file initial size (in gigabytes)

tool.help:
MAF is a text format used at UCSC to store genome alignments. MAFs are typically stored with respect to a reference genome. MAFs can be imported into HAL as subtrees using the maf2hal command.
To import primates.maf as a star tree where the first alignment row specifies the root, and all others the leaves: maf2hal primates.maf primates.hal
To import primates.maf using "chimp" has the root maf2hal primates.maf primates.hal --refGenome chimp
The more the underlying tree looks like a star tree, the less efficient HAL is as all genomes will be fragmented with respect to each other. If ancestral (or multiple reference) sequences are available, or if it is acceptable to use a non-reference species as a reference proxy, then trees of arbitrary typologies can be constructed using the --append option. maf2hal mammals.maf mammals.hal --refGenome mouse --targetGenomes human,rat,chimp,dog maf2hal mammals.maf mammals.hal --append --refGenome human --targetGenomes chimp,gorilla,orang maf2hal mammals.maf mammals.hal --append --refGenome dog --targetGenomes cow,horse
This will create a tree that looks like ((chimp, gorilla,orang)human, rat,(cow,horse)dog)mouse;
**USAGE:** maf2hal [Options] <mafFile> <halFile>
